reason: A contains a factual/math error (calls $12M/$360k ARR "~28x ARR" when it's actually ~33x), which is a minor but real correctness flaw in an argument built on that multiple. Both answers reach the same core recommendation (take it, contingent on wanting the venture path and having a real plan to deploy the capital into growth), both correctly reframe default-alive as changing the calculus from survival to acceleration, and both note ~17% dilution and terms beyond valuation. B does the growth math correctly (8%/mo compounding to ~$75k MRR/year) and more explicitly ties default-alive status to negotiating leverage (suggesting pushing to $14-15M post), landing key points slightly more precisely and concisely than A.
